title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Decision Evidence Lens: Quality Nonclaim
summary: A product passes a familiar unit test but trusts input, overcollects data, leaks failure details, loses work, and has no threat or recovery evidence. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 quality nonclaim increment in trust and quality assurance case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.
Inject timeout, invalid data, dependency failure, interruption, duplicate action, partial write, and restart conditions and verify safe recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Quality Nonclaim · Theory Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
theory case — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Quality Nonclaim contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Quality Nonclaim · Theory Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap. For Predict Quality Nonclaim · Theory Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Quality Nonclaim condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

steps.title: Bound Quality Nonclaim · Theory Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
theory case — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Quality Nonclaim application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.

Evidence target: Bound Quality Nonclaim · Theory Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap. For Bound Quality Nonclaim · Theory Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Quality Nonclaim while producing trust and quality assurance case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The worked example variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap. A defensible solution applies Quality Nonclaim, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to trust and quality assurance case.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-quality-nonclaim — State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-2
focus: pp1-quality-nonclaim
decision-evidence: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality. This fails the Quality Nonclaim contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance (Service Manual current 2026-07-15) — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

The Quality Nonclaim record says trust and quality assurance case looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect quality nonclaim evidence boundary for Quality Nonclaim.

For inspect quality nonclaim evidence, apply Quality Nonclaim without its stated preconditions, omit the trust and quality assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Quality Nonclaim for trust and quality assurance case.

stimulus.title: Quality Nonclaim failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A green continuous-integration run proves production quality.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

options.text: Keep the Quality Nonclaim artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the quality nonclaim defect evidence boundary for Quality Nonclaim.

For repair the quality nonclaim defect, apply Quality Nonclaim without its stated preconditions, omit the trust and quality assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Quality Nonclaim for trust and quality assurance case.
Reconstruct the conditions for Quality Nonclaim, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Defend the Quality Nonclaim model · Theory Quality Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Quality Nonclaim: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.

Evidence target: Defend the Quality Nonclaim model · Theory Quality Nonclaim.
steps.why: Retrieving Quality Nonclaim through the concrete trust and quality assurance case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Quality Nonclaim model · Theory Quality Nonclaim, keep Quality Nonclaim connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State which tests passed, what environment they represent, what remains human or production evidence, and who owns each gap.
